mission.title: falco: Hostname of alert origin not present in HTTP output
mission.description: Hostname of alert origin not present in HTTP output. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current falco deployment
steps.description:
Verify your falco version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
helm list -n falco 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working falco installation.

steps.title: Review falco configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant falco configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get configmap -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=falco
```
**Motivation**

When configuring falco to send alerts to falcosidekick using the falco HTTP output the hostname of the originating machine/node is not present.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Hostname of alert origin not present in HTTP output
steps.description:
Adds `hostname` as a field returned in json output
I'm not normally a cpp developer, please bear with me 🙏 
I'm also definitely missing a docs update somewhere

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get events -n falco --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Hostname of alert origin not present in HTTP output" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Adds `hostname` as a field returned in json output
I'm not normally a cpp developer, please bear with me 🙏 
I'm also definitely missing a docs update somewhere

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with falco installed or the issue environment reproducible.
